devotionals.title: Welcome To Fellowship With the Word
devotionals.verse: John 1:4
devotionals.explanation:
Welcome to Fellowship With The Word. We are preparing daily devotionals to help you grow closer to God every single day. Our full devotional series launches on August 1st, 2026. 
Until then, explore our Bible reader, browse our sermons, and prepare your heart for the journey ahead. The Word of God is alive, and it has the power to transform your life daily.

devotionals.title: Putting on Christ
devotionals.verse: Romans 13:14 NKJV [14] But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to fulfil its lusts.
devotionals.explanation:
It is important to know that there is no identity outside Christ because all are dead outside Christ. 
“In Christ”, marks the beginning of the discovery of identity. When the scripture says to put on the Lord Jesus Christ, it means to sink into the reality of the newness of life that Christ brings to you. The more you discover who you are in Him, the more you realize the responsibility that lies therein. But this responsibility is not from a place of burdens but of ease because it flows from your new nature. When Paul says to make no provision for the flesh, he means to cut off its supply because it has been impaled. It's like trying to retrieve information from a dead person's brain.
There is a thought pattern that is consistent with our new identity, so growth for us is as mentioned in [Ephesians 4:23-24 NLT [23] Instead, let the Spirit renew your thoughts and attitudes. [24] Put on your new nature, created to be like God—truly righteous and holy. Growth for us is in knowledge. So, let's invest heavily in it.
Further study: (Prov 2v1-7) 

PRAYER: Father, thank you for everything that is mind because I am seated In you. I am grateful for your life, for your nature and your character that now finds expression in me through your Spirit. As I interact with your word daily, may I stay conscious of this reality and continue to grow in the consciousness of it all the days of my life. 
Amen!

devotionals.title: You are an heir of God
devotionals.verse: Romans 8:17 AMPC [17] And if we are [His] children, then we are [His] heirs also: heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ [sharing His inheritance with Him]; only we must share His suffering if we are to share His glory.
devotionals.explanation:
The first highlight is that we are His children which comes from the Greek word 'teknon' (His descendants). And as children, we are heirs of God. But when you study the word heir, you realize that the Word for it is 'kleronomos'. That means you partake in a partitioning. You share in an inheritance. And by birth you are a possessor. So, the one who is born again is not empty. You cannot claim to be useless or have nothing to offer. Not only is it a lie, but it’s also a display of ignorance. The responsibility lies in the discovery of the inheritance. But that's not all.
It says we are joint heirs with Christ. Now this is very significant because it gives a pointer to what the inheritance is. So, the question will be... _what did Christ inherit as an heir? Seeing that we are joint heirs with Him. (Romans 4v13, Gal 3v27-29) 

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for the inheritance that I have in you. Thank you for making me an heir and a possessor of all that you have. As your spirit leads, may I continue to walk in the life and authority that you have given to me.
Amen!

devotionals.title: The New Man In Christ
devotionals.verse: Ephesians 2:1 AMP “And you [He made alive when you] were [spiritually] dead and separated from Him because of your transgressions and sins,”
devotionals.explanation:
Have you ever thought about the life of the new man? 
The new man is only new because of Christ. Christ is the beginning, the author and the end of the identity of man. He is the start and the finish of man's identity, such that there's no identity outside Christ. It's important to know that you have bearings in life today because the definition of your life came with Christ. Imagine that the best of men outside Christ is still lost. So, there's nothing to envy about confusion packaged in affluence. What a privilege to be in Christ!
All we have to offer to this world is Christ in us. Christ IN us is the hope of glory. The riches of the knowledge of Christ is more than silver and gold, more than the rubies and pearls, the diamonds and the sapphire and all precious items according to this world's standards. The sum total of them all pales significantly in comparison to the knowledge of Christ. Our sufficiency is of God saints. No wonder Paul said I consider everything else as dung and dregs compared to the knowledge of Christ (Phil 3v8). If we have Christ, we have everything. But the experience of Christ is also a function of knowledge. And so, our fellowship with Him and our experience of the realities in him appreciate as our knowledge increases. For this is life eternal, that we may know him. Discover Christ and you discover you. 
So, the only life worth living on earth is the one characterized by the knowledge of Christ. Give yourself to that cause as long as you have breath. To know him and the power of His resurrection and the fellowship of his sufferings.
PRAYER: Thank you Lord for the life that I have in you, thank you for calling me out of darkness into your marvellous light. Help me to see me the way you see me. May may life always be an expression of your love.
Amen!

devotionals.title: Spiritual Maturity
devotionals.verse: 1 Peter 2:2 As newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the Word, that with it you may grow.
devotionals.explanation:
Today’s word is an encouragement for us to stay on fire and constantly stirred up in the things of God. You see, sometimes you may wonder why you lack motivation to do the things of God or even just pray. But when your true life is informed by the Word, that Word burns within you as zeal, passion or hunger. Remember you're born of the eternal Word (1 Peter 1v23) having been born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, through the word of God, which lives and remains forever, which is Spirit and Life (John 6v63) It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit and are life. 

In the book of 1 Peter 2v2 the apostle says this, as newborn babes, desire the pure milk of the word, that you may grow thereby, Now, this could mean he is talking to spiritual infants and telling them to desire the word to grow. Or it could mean that no matter what level you're at, let your desire for the Word be like that of a tender infant who would cry out for it until its given. But whichever way it's diced, the bottom line is to go for the Word. That is where transformation resides 2 Corinthians 3:18 AMPC And all of us, as with unveiled face, [because we] continued to behold [in the Word of God] as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are constantly being transfigured into His very own image in ever increasing splendor and from one degree of glory to another; [for this comes] from the Lord [Who is] the Spirit. 

PRAYER: Lord, I thank you for this word of encouragement. Thank you for putting in me the desire to know and grow in the knowledge of you. I decree and declare that my desires begin to align with your perfect will and that I stand perfect and complete in all the will of God. In Jesus name I pray, amen.

devotionals.title: God is Not Mysterious To the Believer.
devotionals.verse: I Corinthians 2:9-10 NKJV But as it is written: “Eye has not seen, nor ear heard, nor have entered into the heart of man The things which God has prepared for those who love Him.”  But God has revealed them to us through His Spirit. For the Spirit searches all things, yes, the deep things of God.
devotionals.explanation:
Child of God, it is such a blessing to have the Spirit living in the inside of us. We don't get tired of being taught of the Spirit. The Holy Spirit uncovers the mystery of God which is Christ. So, God is not mysterious to the believer. 
He has revealed himself to the believer in the person of Jesus Christ who now lives in us in the person of the Holy Spirit.
I Corinthians 2:11-12 NKJV [11] For what man knows the things of a man except the spirit of the man which is in him? Even so no one knows the things of God except the Spirit of God. [12] Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit who is from God, that we might know the things that have been freely given to us by God.

We are not trying to figure out the mind of God or what God is saying because the word tells us that we HAVE the MIND of Christ to be guided by his thoughts and purposes. Glory to God!
PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for the outpour of your love to me, thank you for giving me everything I need for life and godliness and thank you for giving me your mind. I do not struggle to discern your will but I discern and fulfil your will with ease and with excitement. 
Amen!

devotionals.title: God’s Presence is Always with You Independent of Circumstances.
devotionals.verse: : Exodus 3:11-12 NLT  [11] But Moses protested to God, “Who am I to appear before Pharaoh? Who am I to lead the people of Israel out of Egypt?” [12] God answered, “I will be with you. And this is your sign that I am the one who has sent you: When you have brought the people out of Egypt, you will worship God at this very mountain.”
devotionals.explanation:
When Moses doubted his identity and ability, the first thing God assured him of was his presence with him. It is something that is common through scripture. In Psalm 23v4 David even reveals his confidence and assurance in His presence with him, independent of circumstances.
Psalms 23:4 NKJV [4] Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil; For You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me.

If you ever made a conscious decision at accepting the life of God, let there be a wiring within you that he is always with you and to take it a step further, he lives in you.
Find confidence and assurance in his presence in you and with you. He is everything and more. An inheritance Incorruptible. Shalom!

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, your presence with me makes all the difference. Thank you for giving me an assurance that you will never leave me nor forsake me. Thank you for being the one who stays when everyone else leaves. I am content in your love for me and I rest knowing that your presence is always with me. Amen!

devotionals.title: Your Life Is Hidden in Christ in God
devotionals.verse: Colossians 3:3 NLT For you died to this life, and your real life is hidden with Christ in God.
devotionals.explanation:
The beauty in knowing your identity is that it reveals even your ability in and through God. The moment the light bulb turns on about your true nature as love, you cease to depend on motivation or circumstances to walk in love. The moment you realize that you're perfectly righteous in the sight of God because of Jesus’ sacrifice, you cease to think that your efforts or lack thereof make you acceptable in His sight. When you realize that the power that rose Christ from the dead (Eph 1v19-20) is the same power that works in you (Eph 3v20), you begin to see possibilities beyond human ability. 

When you realize that the same body that seemingly is the cause of the sin that so easily entangles, had been rendered useless and impotent as touching sin (better put, crucified), you notice you don't have to stay bound. 

Consider yourself dead to all fears and alive to the realities made available for you in Christ. Christ is your life (Col 3v4). It is clear that knowledge is the light that wipes the darkness away. You're stronger than you realize, because you're the best and ultimate version of God's creativity (Eph 2v10). How could you possibly be disadvantaged? Square your shoulders, focus on God and move on.

PRAYER: Father, thank you for giving me a new nature and a new identity in Christ Jesus. Thank you for your love that has been shed abroad in my heart through the Holy Spirit that was given to me. I walk in boldness and in the freedom that you already won for me, in Jesus name. Amen

Further study: Ephesians 1:19-20, Ephesians 3:20, Ephesians 2:10.

devotionals.title: Your Performance Is Not What Keeps You in Right Relationship with God
devotionals.verse: 1 Corinthians 5:21 God made Him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf, so that in Him we might become the righteousness of God.
devotionals.explanation:
Remember that your performance and good behaviour is not what is keeping you in right relationship with God right now. Neither are the mistakes that you've made keeping you in bad relationship with Him. Good before God is a product of a good nature. One that is renewed. So too is bad. If you believe and confess the Lord Jesus, you are good before God. The reason is because you have completely depended on the obedience of Jesus more than yours to please God. 
Romans 5:17 NLT [17] For the sin of this one man, Adam, caused death to rule over many. But even greater is God’s wonderful grace and his gift of righteousness, for all who receive it will live in triumph over sin and death through this one man, Jesus Christ. 

When God sees you, he sees Jesus. He took your place in sin, so you can have His place and enjoy the relationship he had with God. 2 Corinthians 5:21 MSG [21] How? you ask. In Christ. God put the wrong on him who never did anything wrong, so we could be put right with God. Please stop being confident in your ability to influence how God sees you. He loves you for nothing. That's how good He is. Remember that you have a nature that causes you to do right. Let that be the truth you accept so that you can live that way. Walking with God is not complicated.

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for taking away the nature of sin and giving me a nature of righteousness. Thank you because your love for me is not dependent on my actions but solely on what you did on the cross. Because of you I am holy, righteous, justified and set apart to do good works. Your word is my reality, and I accept everything you’ve said about me with meekness and humility. Amen!

devotionals.title: You Have the Mind of Christ.
devotionals.verse: 1 Corinthians 2:12 And we have received God’s Spirit (not the world’s spirit), so we can know the wonderful things God has freely given us.
devotionals.explanation:
Because of God's impartial desire for every one of his children to know him, He gave us his Spirit. And as you read through let your deep-seated consideration be that THIS SPIRIT LIVES IN ME WITH ALL HE HAS TO OFFER. The Holy Spirit in us gives us the capacity to understand the word. He’s the revealer of the mind of God and the things freely given to us. He is God's compass to you. He is the custodian of the mind and reasoning of God. 

The scripture says that no man knows the things of God except the Spirit of God. 1 Corinthians 2:11 TPT puts it like this [11] After all, who can really see into a person’s heart and know his hidden impulses except for that person’s spirit? 
So, it is with God. His thoughts and secrets are only fully understood by his Spirit, the Spirit of God.  But what better news could there be than that we received the Spirit which is of God so we might KNOW the things FREELY GIVEN to us of God?

1 Corinthians 2:12 TPT [12] For we did not receive the spirit of this world system but the Spirit of God, so that we might come to understand and experience all that grace has lavished upon us. These are the things which we speak and preach, of course not with words which man's wisdom teaches but with words the Holy Ghost teaches. And natural men cannot receive it because they are spiritually discerned and not carnally discerned. It is foolishness to them. But because you're born of the spirit, your born spiritually perceptive. 

You're not a natural man. And we have the mind of Christ (1 Cor 2v16). We have his perceptions and computations. The understanding of Christ. Please I'd like us to catch this or see it deeper... if you have the Spirit of God in you, have access. You are abundantly rich in grace, in peace, in mercy from God, in faith, in life(zoe) and much more. You have the perceptions of Christ. 

2 Peter 1:2 AMPC [2] May grace (God's favor) and peace (which is perfect well-being, all necessary good, all spiritual prosperity, and freedom from fears and agitating passions and moral conflicts) be multiplied to you in [the full, personal, precise, and correct] knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord. All of God riches abound to you in knowledge. 

PRAYER: Dear Lord Jesus, when I contemplate the extent of your extravagant love for me, I am filled with so much gratitude. Thank you for eternal life, thank you for your supernatural life at work in me and thank me for giving me your mind so that I can know you. As I grow in the knowledge of you, I see myself and my heart burns with more love for you. Be always glorified in my life. In Jesus name I pray, amen.

devotionals.title: The Hallmark of Christian Maturity
devotionals.verse: Ephesians 1:13 NKJV “In Him you also trusted, after you heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation; in whom also, having believed, you were sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise.”
devotionals.explanation:
Grace and peace to you. Something to contemplate on as you go about the day. The Holy Ghost is all we need for life and godliness given by God. He is all that God has to offer. He is God's gift to humanity and much more.

The sum of all the riches in the Heavenly in Christ, now dwelling in you. And He's more than enough. He is the guarantee of our inheritance in Christ. 
Let's renew our minds to being comfortable with the sufficiency of the Holy Spirit. It takes away expressions of greed and focuses on contentment and gratitude. Once we catch this, something will burst forth in our love walk - the hallmark of our Christian maturity. 
1 Corinthians 14:1 NLT [1] Let love be your highest goal!... 

PRAYER: Father, thank you for the gift of the Holy Spirit now dwelling in me. I am grateful for his companionship, partnership, friendship, leadership and authority in my life. I yield to his promptings, and I fulfil your will for my life. Amen!

devotionals.title: There Is No Condemnation In Christ
devotionals.verse: John 8:10-11 NKJV “When Jesus had raised Himself up and saw no one but the woman, He said to her, “Woman, where are those accusers of yours? Has no one condemned you?” She said, “No one, Lord.” And Jesus said to her, “Neither do I condemn you; go and sin no more.”
devotionals.explanation:
In John 8v1-11, Jesus has an encounter with the pharisees, and the woman caught in adultery. You see, whenever we're studying and we come across anything that Jesus is involved with, let everything in you focus and give attention. That's because as the perfect expression of the mind and reasoning of God, there's always an excellent example to renew our minds with. 

God doesn't condemn anyone because there is no condemnation in Christ. (Read John 3v17 & Romans 8v1) so he's not mad at anyone no matter what. However, right after the encounter with the woman caught in adultery, Jesus is addressing the crowd and the Pharisees are among the same people that brought the woman before him. He said something to them in John 8:15 NLT [15] You judge me by human standards, but I do not judge anyone. (or you judge just by what you see). I do not judge anyone. But even if I do judge, my judgment is right.

These pharisees had written off this lady and wanted her alone to be condemned because she was caught in adultery. A good question to ask would be... How easy is it for us to see people for who God sees them to be, beyond what the external appearances portray? How often do we scrutinize and evaluate and pass judgment at first sight based on appearance? In the same way that not everyone who is suited is a saint, not everyone who is tattered is a thug. 

The Scripture says man looks on the outward appearance and God looks at the heart. 1 Samuel 16:7 NLT [7] But the Lord said to Samuel, “Don’t judge by his appearance or height, for I have rejected him. The Lord doesn’t see things the way you see them. People judge by outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.”

That tells you that there is something about the standard of human judgment that inevitably falls short of Gods standard. Statistics say that the average adult makes about 35000 decisions a day. If you think about how many times you were aware about Gods standards when making any of the decisions, how many can boast of up to 10 or 15. Jeremiah 10:23 ESV [23] I know, O Lord, that the way of man is not in himself, that it is not in man who walks to direct his steps. 

The Holy Spirit you received when you believed the gospel was given to God's you into all truth. He lives in you to lead you in the way you should go and to reveal to you the mind of God who is Christ. He wants to shape the way you think by his word. Always remember that Christ is the standard of your decision-making process. So, to make better decisions in life, invest yourself in the knowledge of Christ. 

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for the word that has come to me today. Help me to say others the way you see them and help me to love like you do. I submit to the leadership of the Holy Spirit for the transformation and renewal of my mind to yours, in Jesus name. Amen!

devotionals.title: Repentance
devotionals.verse: Romans 2:4 NKJV [4] Or do you despise the riches of His goodness, forbearance, and longsuffering, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?
devotionals.explanation:
The word repentance is translated from the Greek word metanoia. It is from two other Greek words “Meta” - meaning succession, afterward, Change, and “Nous” or “noeo” - meaning mind, comprehension or understanding. It therefore means to THINK DIFFERENTLY or RECONSIDER. 

So how does God bring about repentance in his children? Is it through Confession of sins or other way? Is it possible to repent without committing sin? 

Romans 2:4 NKJV [4] Or do you despise the riches of His goodness, forbearance, and longsuffering, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance? 

Repentance is a product of the revelation of God's goodness. It is a message of faith toward God that causes you to change how you think about God and how you respond to his will. It's not confession of sins. Or saying you're sorry. When next you feel like you're sorry about something and think to yourself that God is angry, your corresponding action is not confession. 

It's also important to make the difference between godly sorrow and guilt. Both are a function of the training of our conscience. (1 John 3v20) Confession of sins doesn't make a difference with God in any way. What changes men is the revelation of God's goodness. When one is busy saying they are sorry, God is busy seeking for multiple ways to expose you to light and more truth about his love and goodness. Because that’s the way he changes men. 
For your actions to change, your thinking must change. Not your sorrow. 

PRAYER: Lord, I thank you for your mercy, love and grace. Thank you for being patient with me all the while and thank you for never giving up on me. I renew my mind with the entrance of your word, and I walk in the fullness of life that you’ve given to me.
Amen!

devotionals.title: All Your Sins Have Been Forgiven by God
devotionals.verse: Hebrews 8:12 NKJV For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness, and their sins and their lawless deeds I will remember no more.”
devotionals.explanation:
When something goes wrong, is your first thought, "Well, this must be happening to me because of the sins that I have committed"? When you have a flat tire or fall sick, do you wonder, "What sin is God punishing me for now?" If your answer to both questions is "yes," you are not alone. 
This kind of thinking is so prevalent in the church because many believers don't really believe that they are under the new covenant. They believe the opposite of today's Scripture and think God is not merciful to their mistakes and always mindful of their sins. The problem with the church today is wrong believing. 
Jesus Himself defined the new covenant for us at the last supper when He said, "This is My blood of the new covenant, which is shed for many for the remission of sins" (Matt. 26:28). The main clause of the new covenant is the forgiveness of all your sins because of the shed blood of Jesus Christ. Your forgiveness does not hinge on how many good deeds you have done, how much money you have given to the church or charities, or what leadership position you hold. No, it all hinges on the shed blood of Jesus. That final clause is what makes the new covenant work in our lives. If you don't believe the main and final clause, then you won't experience the full benefits of the new covenant. You are also negating God's Word and the finished work of Jesus. The new covenant says that God is merciful to your unrighteousness and has forgotten your sins and lawless deeds. If God says that He has forgotten them, then He has truly forgotten them. God cannot lie!
But how can God forget my sins? He can because He is God! If He said it, then He has done it. You know that sin you committed many years ago? God has forgotten it. He does not keep an itemized account of all your failures. There is no big projector screen in heaven to show all your sins—from the day you were born to the day you die. All records of your sins have been incinerated by the blood of Jesus when He cried out, "It is finished!" (John 19:30).

When God looks at you today, He sees you covered with Jesus' blood and completely righteous. Only the devil, you yourself, and the people around you will bring your sins to your remembrance. So, when you are weighed down by the mistakes of your past, remind yourself that you live in Christ and lean on His grace! He's not about to leave you or forsake you. Why? For He will be merciful to your unrighteousness, and your sins and lawless deeds He will remember no more. This is the main clause of the new covenant of grace! This is His Word! 

PRAYER: Father, thank you for forgiving me past, present and future sins. Thank you for giving Jesus on my behalf. I believe your word and I accept it over my life. I walk in the newness of life that you’ve given to me in Christ.
Amen!

devotionals.title: The Promised Gift of God To Mankind
devotionals.verse: Galatians 3:14 NKJV “That the blessing of Abraham might come upon the Gentiles in Christ Jesus, that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.”
devotionals.explanation:
The greatest and singular treasure (gift) of God is the Holy Spirit. Recognize how much he loves you to have chosen to share Himself with you. In Galatians 3:13-14 ESV [13] Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us—for it is written, “Cursed is everyone who is hanged on a tree” [14] so that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might come to the Gentiles, so that we might receive the promised Spirit through faith. 

The latter part of what we just read helps us to understand that the ultimate blessing of Abraham wasn't diamonds, gold, land, or cattle; it was the Spirit. Through Jesus Christ the promise of the Spirit has now come to us who believe. He lives in us, works through us, walks in us, and empowers us daily. 

This is the greatest treasure of the New Testament: The Holy Spirit in you! He's the seal of our inheritance, the proof that we belong to God. He's not a shadow or a force: He's a divine Person who walks with you and lives in you. His presence is what makes the Christian life supernatural, victorious and exciting. We need to be more aware of His power that's at work within us. 

Ephesians 1:19-20 talks about the exceeding greatness of His mighty power at work in you: 'And what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us-ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power, Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead...."Hallelujah! 

There's no need to pray for power, strength, or wisdom because the Holy Spirit, who is Himself wisdom, strength, and the source of all power and divine guidance, is already resident in you. You're never without help. The same Spirit that raised the Lord Jesus from the dead lives in you. This is the fulfilment of God's eternal plan. The prophets longed to see it. The patriarchs anticipated it. But you're living it: "Even the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations, but now is made manifest to his saints...which is Christ in you, the hope of glory" (Colossians 1:26-27)

Also, in 1 Peter 1:10-12 ESV [10] Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace that was to be yours searched and inquired carefully, [11] inquiring what person or time the Spirit of Christ in them was indicating when he predicted the sufferings of Christ and the subsequent glories. [12] It was revealed to them that they were serving not themselves but you, in the things that have now been announced to you through those who preached the good news to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven, things into which angels long to look. 
Christianity without the Holy Spirit is religion. But with Him, it's a divine adventure. He makes the Word come alive, fills your prayer with power, and transforms you from glory to glory. Acknowledge Him, Yield to Him, and fellowship with Him always. He's the ultimate blessing of the New Testament. 

PRAYER: Father, thank you for the gift of your Spirit to me. Thank you for this priceless gift and the supernatural life that I have because I host your spirit. May my daily living reflect a consciousness of this reality as I fulfil all that you’ve called me to fulfil. I love you Lord and I am grateful that you chose me independent of anything I could ever do. Be glorified in my life. Amen!

devotionals.title: Salvation (The Gift of God)
devotionals.verse: Titus 3:3-8
devotionals.explanation:
In Titus 3:3-8 NLT scripture says [3] Once we, too, were foolish and disobedient. We were misled and became slaves to many lusts and pleasures. Our lives were full of evil and envy, and we hated each other. [4] But — When God our Savior revealed his kindness and love, [5] he saved us, not because of the righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He washed away our sins, giving us a new birth and new life through the Holy Spirit. [6] He generously poured out the Spirit upon us through Jesus Christ our Savior. [7] Because of his grace he made us right in his sight and gave us confidence that we will inherit eternal life. [8] This is a trustworthy saying, and I want you to insist on these teachings so that all who trust in God will devote themselves to doing good. These teachings are good and beneficial for everyone.

Salvation may have become cliche in many circles. But it is the revelation of the God's love, kindness and goodness to mankind independent of anything man can do. If you don't understand salvation, you can't understand God's love, and if you can't understand God's love you can't love right. But remember Paul's charge in 1 Corinthians 14:1 NLT [1] Let love be your highest goal!

When the apostle Paul was training the early churches, this is what he asked the leaders to emphasize. And given that we don't get creative with the faith that was handed down to us, let's equally insist on this subject. It is by these teachings that men devote themselves to doing good. Let's invest ourselves in learning salvation. Every effort at learning salvation is an effort towards learning God's heart, God's will and God's thinking process or mindset.

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for this wonderful gift of salvation. Your word says that it is your will for all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth. This is the reason you went to the cross. May I be preoccupied with the things that matter to you. May I invest in knowing more about this gift that I have received and with this knowledge disciple and raise men for you. In Jesus name, Amen.

devotionals.title: What Is Gods Will?
devotionals.verse: I Timothy 2:3-4 NKJV “For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior, who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.”
devotionals.explanation:
When we talk about God's will, what do we even mean? The word will is from the Greek 'thelema', which means one's choice, purpose, determination, or inclination. It is the pleasure or desire of someone. So, God's will represent God's choice, his purpose, his determinations, and inclinations. It will also represent his pleasures and desires. God has expressed his desires and pleasures, his choice, purposes, and inclinations to us in his Word. 

But when we say in his word, we mean in the revelation of Jesus. Jesus was the visible manifestation of God's will here on earth. That is why he said, "This is my beloved Son in whom I am WELL PLEASED."(Matt 3v17).

So when you look at God's pleasure and desire, it is captured in salvation when Paul in 1Tim 2:3-4 expresses his pleasure first for all men to be saved, then to come to the full recognition and knowledge of Jesus. 
When you look at his inclinations, it is to His nature of love, so Paul says in Eph 5v1-2 to be imitators of God as you walk in love. When you look at his purposes and determinations, in Eph 2v10 Paul writes that [10] ...we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand that we should walk in them. That is still dealing with his will. 
When you come to the knowledge of God, you'd realize that his will is for you to no longer live for yourself but for him who died for you as captured in Galatians 2:20 NKJV. 

II Corinthians 5:14-15 NKJV [14] For the love of Christ compels us, because we judge thus: that if One died for all, then all died; [15] and He died for all, that those who live should live no longer for themselves, but for Him who died for them and rose again. 
This understanding informs your submission to and execution of the good works that He has predetermined for us in Christ before the foundation of the world. Those good works are the specific things that culminate to form your purpose. It is this execution that is the participation with God in His plan. 

However, on a daily basis, how does all this translate even to every decision you take? Jesus said My sheep hear my voice, I know them, and they follow. (John 10v26) By the reality of the new birth in Jesus, God's will has been wired into our spirits when his Spirit came to live in our spirit and became one with ours. (see Ezek 11v19, 36v26, Jer 31v33, Heb 8v10).
First settle it in you that all children of God hear God's voice. Most times, we don't discern even when we hear His voice because of distraction and ignorance. That is where the importance of always praying and fellowshipping comes in. Because in prayer and fellowship, focus and Communication come in because of the influence of God's Spirit.

The scripture says the Spirit of God teaches us all things (1 John 2v27). In the teaching process, your mind is renewed, or your manner of thinking changes. And that change in thought pattern positions you to approve what God's will is, which is good, acceptable, and perfect. The more understanding you have, and the more transformed your mind is about Jesus through fellowship, the more awareness of his will you attain, and the easier and more confident you will be in participation in it.

PRAYER: Father, thank you for revealing your will to me in the person of Jesus Christ. You are more interested in my growth in the knowledge of you more than I could ever be and for that, I am grateful. I pray that this knowledge informs every decision that I make, and every action that I take. Thank you because my steps are ordered by you. I yield to your leading and direction and I depend on you to do the works that have been written in the volume of the books for my life. In jesus name I pray, amen.
Further studies: Galatians 2:20, 1 John 2:27, Hebrews 8:10, Ephesians 2:10.

devotionals.title: The Will of God
devotionals.verse: I Timothy 2:3-4 NKJV “For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior, who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.”
devotionals.explanation:
God’s will is for all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth (1 Tim 2v4). This captures the full weight of God's will. Salvation and knowledge of Him. For all men to be saved, and for all men to come to the knowledge of the truth, there must be preaching.

 Romans 10:13-14 ESV it is put like this [13] For “everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.” [14] How then will they call on him in whom they have not believed? And how are they to believe in him of whom they have never heard? And how are they to hear without someone preaching? 

It's very central to the execution of God's will. That's why the charge Jesus gave before leaving, was to go into all the world and make disciples of the nations. To teach and make students of the nations. Letting them know to observe the things He spoke (Mtt 28v19-20). 
Paul also echoes this when he said in Titus 1:1-3 ESV when he says that the calling he received from God is for the elect to come to the knowledge of the truth and it is through preaching. So already we can see how he's preaching for men to be saved (Romans 10v13-14) and for the elect to come to the knowledge of the truth (Titus 1v1-3). 

As children of God, whatever it is that we think we're called to do has to have this as underlying factor. What's the benefit of being a doctor saving physical lives that still end in hell? Or a firefighter rescuing people who end up condemned? 

Child of God, Know the message, let it influence your life and speak it out. You are the light that the dark world is waiting for. Jesus said... John 9:5 ESV [5] As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.” Also, in John 8:12 ESV [12] Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness but will have the light of life.” 
And Matthew 5:16 ESV [16] In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven. So please don't hold back. We have something to offer by the foolishness of preaching. We administer life as ambassadors of Christ. 

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for your word that has come to me today. Thank you for revealing your will and the thing that matters most to you. I partner with your Spirit to do the work of ministry. I preach the message of your kingdom to the ends of the earth, and I disciple men to do the work of ministry, until all come to know you. I declare that through my life, men will receive the light of life. In Jesus name I pray, amen.

devotionals.title: Loving Like Christ Jesus
devotionals.verse: Romans 12:9-10 NKJV [9] Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor what is evil. Cling to what is good. [10] Be kindly affectionate to one another with brotherly love, in honor giving preference to one another.
devotionals.explanation:
Romans 12:9-10 NKJV [9] Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor what is evil. Cling to what is good. [10] Be kindly affectionate to one another with brotherly love, in honor giving preference to one another. 

The verse 10 communicates a disposition we should maintain towards fellow believers which was translated in English as kindly affectionate. But we may not fully grasp what that truly means just by looking on the surface. The word used there was the Greek 'philostorgos' which is to be fraternal towards Christians. From two words; 'philos' (meaning 'dear' as used with a friend. Also means to be fond of or friendly with sm1), and 'storgē' (familial love and fondness that exists between siblings or family relations or a couple). 

Storgē is not used in the Bible in any verse but to understand it we can look at the opposite of philostorgos. The opposite of philostorgos can be found in the Bible: 'astorgous', used in the Greek translation of Scripture to mean ‘without natural affection,’ and seen in Romans 1v31. The word “astorgous” is translated as “heartless or hard hearted.”

The essence of the brief word study is to arrive at an encouragement from the instruction. It is to be as disposed and available to other believers as you would be to your family of blood. And to be very warm and considerate and kind to them as you are toward your family. V9 said love should be without hypocrisy. Don't measure your love and cherry pick who you'll love more because of fleshy evaluations. We are instructed in the word to Know no one after the flesh. You make no difference loving only those who love you, or who you consider deserving of your love.

I beg and beseech you to act on this truth. Begin to be much more sensitive to the needs of others. Love is not consumed with oneself. Let your words be gracious and stand for truth in love. 

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for being the perfect example of love and for laying down your life for me. Thank you because your word says that your love has been shed abroad in my heart through the Holy Spirit that was given to me. I do not struggle to love because I received your nature which is love. Thank you because I demonstrate this love to the people around me and to the world at large. I declare that through my life, men will come to understand the love of God. I love you Jesus and I am grateful for this privilege. In Jesus name I pray, Amen.

devotionals.title: The Goodnews
devotionals.verse: “For He made Him who knew no sin to be sin for us, that we might become the righteousness of God in Him.” II Corinthians 5:21 NKJV
devotionals.explanation:
The discovery of and belief in God's righteousness revealed in Christ is the power that saves men. That discovery can only be made in the gospel. It is the avenue of the expression of God's love. So, we can say that the gospel is the expose of God's ability to uphold justice to its pristine standard (by dealing with sin in Christ), while displaying unmatched and unquantifiable love simultaneously (by raising us from the dead in Christ). The proclamation of this freedom we've obtained and the new life in Christ is the gospel. Paul coined it this way... 
Romans 1:16-17 NKJV [16] For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ, for it is the power of God to salvation for everyone who believes, for the Jew first and also for the Greek. [17] For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith; as it is written, “The just shall live by faith.”

Learn to see God's intentionality in the pages of scripture. See His love in all his actions. Your fellowship gets better with that. Please think deeply about these words, they are not superficial. They'll bless you.

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for your extravagant love and grace. Thank you for crediting your righteousness to my account and making me a new creature. Lord, I see you and more In the written word, I see myself and I am transformed to become increasingly like you. As I give myself to the ministry of the word and prayer, may this light in me shine so bright that men in darkness will come to the knowledge of you. In Jesus name I pray, amen.

devotionals.title: Your Flesh Was Crucified With Christ
devotionals.verse: Colossians 3:8-10 NKJV [8] But now you yourselves are to put off all these: anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy language out of your mouth. [9] Do not lie to one another, since you have put off the old man with his deeds, [10] and have put on the new man who is renewed in knowledge according to the image of Him who created him.
devotionals.explanation:
There is a misconception when among believers we say things like "we're fighting or killing the flesh". Our fight as believers is not "the flesh". It is ignorance. The flesh died when you said yes to Jesus. There are several interesting words to investigate from the verses above. 

The first is the word "put off". It's used in verse 8 and verse 9. Interestingly, different Greek words are used in the different verses. This is one reason you don't want to approach your Bible like you do with another English textbook or history material. In verse 8, the Greek used is 'apotithēmi' which means -to put away or to lay aside or to cast off. So, Paul is telling the church to put away or lay aside or cast off (separate themselves) from every anger, filthy communication, malice etc.

In verse 9 however, the Greek for the same English word that was used is interesting. It is "apekduomai". It means -to despoil or to divest wholly oneself. Okay, to divest means to strip someone of property, rights, or authority; it means to relinquish control. Similar to divest, despoil means to plunder, or to strip someone or something of possessions, value, or attractiveness, often by force, robbery, or ruthless destruction, leaving it ruined.

It is the same word used in Colossians 2:15 NKJV [15] Having disarmed principalities and powers, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them in it. So, if you think about what Paul is saying in our earlier v9, he's telling them to not lie to one another (or sin in general) because their reality is that their flesh (that old man) has been disarmed. He was crucified. 

Romans 6:6 KJV [6] knowing this, that our old man is crucified with him (Christ), that the body of sin might be destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve sin. Galatians 5:24 KJV [24] And they that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts. 

So please there are two charges for us, First is this Romans 6:11-12 TPT [11] So let it be the same way with you! Since you are now joined with him, you must continually view yourselves as dead and unresponsive to sin’s appeal while living daily for God’s pleasure in union with Jesus, the Anointed One.12] Sin is a dethroned monarch; so you must no longer give it an opportunity to rule over your life, controlling how you live and compelling you to obey its desires and cravings.

Second is this I Corinthians 15:34 NKJV [34] Awake to righteousness, and do not sin; for some do not have the knowledge of God_ ... Please think on these things and act on it. These are priceless truths that will change our lives for ever. 

PRAYER: Lord Jesus, thank you for your word that has come to me today. I receive the word of your grace with excitement and meekness and i give room for your Spirit to transform and renew my mind. Your word is the final authority and my reality and i accept and walk in the reality of this word. Amen!
